{"key_event":"Liberation and postwar republican reconstruction","reasoning":"By 1948, French state sovereignty had been restored and expanded through planning, nationalizations, social security, rationing, and technocratic administration, though Fourth Republic fragmentation and scarcity kept it below overwhelming capacity. Civil society recovered from Vichy repression through the Resistance legacy, women’s suffrage, mass parties, unions, and strikes, but Cold War polarization limited further consolidation.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":8.6,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.7}
